edibility: Toxicity disputed among sources; many classify as inedible or poisonous; never recommended for consumption due to potential gastrointestinal effects and inconsistent toxicity reports.
taste: Mild flavor, though consumed rarely. Notable characteristic: flesh easily blackens with bruising or handling, darkening to jet-black over time.
search: Found in old grasslands and meadows worldwide, particularly in Europe and North America. Key identification clue: bruises and aged caps turn distinctly jet-black. Main look-alikes include other Hygrocybe species (scarlet waxcap, parrot waxcap) which differ in color and habitat preference.

toxicity.symptoms: Reports of gastrointestinal distress in some individuals; toxicity is not consistently reported across all cases
toxicity.treatmentWindow: Early intervention with supportive care recommended
toxicity.antidote: Supportive care, hydration, and symptomatic treatment of gastrointestinal symptoms
